methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $6.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Zipline
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series G)

data.narrative:
Nuro is a Series G autonomous delivery drone company founded by Dave Ferguson and Jiajun Zhu. The sector encompasses four tracked private peers—Joby Aviation, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Zipline—with no direct public comparables available. The narrowness of the peer set constrains multi-method triangulation.

Stage-progression methodology is the applicable valuation lever. Starting from Nuro's last post-money valuation of $6.0B, application of a typical Series G step-up multiplier of 1.25x yields $7.5B. Peer-median analysis was not viable; while the Drones & Autonomous sector contains three peers, none meet minimum thresholds (≥3 same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier datapoints) required for statistical reliability. Deploying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable cross-sector noise.

The triangulated valuation range supported by applicable methods is $6.4B (low) to $8.6B (high), with a median of $7.5B. This range reflects a modest step-up from current post-money, consistent with late-stage venture progression but appropriately conservative given sector-specific peer scarcity. Key uncertainties include the lack of public market pricing signals and limited private comparables at Series G scale in autonomous delivery. Path to revenue materiality and regulatory milestones remain material inputs not captured in valuation mechanics alone.
